extraction.one_line_summary: PINOT Thibaut attacked with 6km remaining on Lagos de Covadonga, gaining 28 seconds as GC favorites marked each other instead of chasing.
tactical_patterns: Pinot exploited GC paranoia by attacking from outside podium threat range; favorites let him gain 28s while marking each other.
Astana paced category one climb mid-stage despite no immediate GC rider, revealing multi-day fatigue management strategy before rest day.
Lopez's late attack triggered Yates to chase down Quintana/Valverde cooperation, fragmenting any unified response to Pinot's move.

rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: attacked from deficit position knowing GC men wouldn't chase, won by 28s, moved into podium calculation
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: got frustrated marking Quintana/Valverde instead of controlling Pinot's dangerous move from 6km out
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: neutral: strong enough to attack late but tactical timing poor, only gained seconds on rivals while Pinot took 28s
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: dropped initially then returned, suggesting vulnerability on sustained steep gradients despite podium position
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: positive: stayed with elite GC group despite not being pre-race favorite, matching Quintana/Masse on final climb
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: neutral: breakaway role suggests Jumbo supporting teammate rather than sprinting, tactical flexibility shown
rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Dylan: negative: dropped on Lagos approach with 20km remaining, signaling insufficient climbing for Asturian mountain stages
